Assembly: The assembly daCirErio1.hap1.1 is based on 40x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly records heterozygous inversions on Chromosome 9 (~34.1-43 Mbp) and Chromosome 16 (~49.4-56.6 Mbp).

Assembly: The assembly ilAnaVerb1.hap1.1 is based on 79x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).The exact order and orientation of the contigs on the telomeric regions of chromosome 28 (0-1.6 Mbp and 9.5 Mbp - end) is unknown.

Assembly: The assembly ilDicFasc1.hap1.1 is based on 67x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome was identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 13 (0-1.12 Mbp) is unknown. The duplication at chromosome 2 47.8Mbp is supported by coverage and the alternate haplotype.

Assembly: The assembly dcOxyDigy1.hap1.1 is based on 216x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Assembly: The assembly icLocCrat2.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The following regions of this assembly are of undetermined order and orientation: Chromosome 9 region 37.4 - End Mbp, Chromosome Y.

Assembly: The assembly qdTypLobi1.hap1.1 is based on 31x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of scaffolds within the following regions is uncertain: Chromosome 1 between approximately 20.97-76.37Mb Chromosome 2 between approximately 24.36-58.04Mb Chromosome 3 between approximately 27.29-50.90Mb Chromosome 4 between approximately 38.17-49.74Mb Chromosome 5 from the start to approximately 32.83Mb Chromosome 6 between approximately 42.17-47.45Mb Chromosome 7 between approximately 20.53-29.70Mb Inversions between haplotypes were observed in the following locations: Chromosome 1 between approximately 98.76-104.39Mb Chromosome 2 between approximately 70.2-100.2Mb Chromosome 3 between approximately 12.06-26.96Mb.
